growth_records.medium: Modified Frey's broth (mycoplasma broth, MB) + 10% swine serum

up.method: GC-MS of culture supernatant, late-log cells resuspended in fresh MB, sampled 0-240 min (Fig 5A)
[U-13C]glucose (1 mM) steady-state labeling, GC-MS of intracellular metabolites (Fig 4A)

mfa.note: Efficient exchange of intracellular/extracellular glucose but low flux into downstream glycolysis/PPP; authors infer glucose is largely not catabolized and other C sources (glycerol/lactate) preferred. Not a mmol/gDW/h flux.
13C from glycerol incorporated into G3P and hexose phosphates indicating active gluconeogenesis / glycerol catabolism in M. bovis. No absolute flux reported.
M. bovis nets consumes lactate from medium (Fig 5C); consistent with preference for non-hexose carbon sources. No absolute flux.

growth_records.cite: Masukagami Y et al. Comparative Metabolomics of Mycoplasma bovis and Mycoplasma gallisepticum Reveals Fundamental Differences in Active Metabolic Pathways and Suggests Novel Gene Annotations. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00055-17. [PMC5634790]
growth_records.method: not_reported (growth monitored qualitatively vs prior growth curves and by phenol-red color-change MPN/CCU; no mu or td value reported)
growth_records.snippet: [13C]glucose was effectively taken up by M. bovis, as shown by efficient steady-state labeling of the intracellular glucose pool (78%). However, only low levels of label were detected in downstream glycolytic and pentose phosphate pathway i
growth_records.notes: 13C-metabolomics/tracer paper (not classical INST-MFA); NO absolute uptake/secretion rates (no mmol/gDW/h), NO growth rate, doubling time, biomass yield, or gDW normalization reported. Cell numbers standardized to 1x10^10 CCU for extraction. Uptake/depletion values in Fig 5 are GC-MS peak abundances relative to t=0 (not absolute concentrations); 13C values are % enrichment. Medium composition fully stated (Frey's broth, pH 8.1, 37 C, aerobic, batch). All captured rates are experimental measurements but directional/relative only; no derived qS possible from stated numbers.
